name: Gratin of Greens Casserole by James Beard, Chef
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash the spinach well and remove the coarse stalks. Dry the leaves thoroughly and chop them fine.
2. Cook the spinach in 3/4 cup olive oil over medium heat until just wilted. Drain and put into a large bowl.
3. Wash, dry, and chop the Swiss chard. Cook it in another 3/4 cup olive oil until wilted. Drain and add to the spinach.
4. Wash, dry, trim, and dice the zucchini. Add about 2 tablespoons olive oil to the skillet. Sauté the zucchini until just tender and add to the spinach and chard mixture.
5. Season with salt to taste and mix in the cooked rice and garlic.
6. Transfer the mixture to a well-oiled, heavy 2-quart baking dish. Spoon additional olive oil over the top.
7. Bake at 300°F for 20 minutes. Beat the eggs and pour them over the vegetable mixture. Sprinkle with bread crumbs.
8. Return to the oven and bake until the eggs are just set, about 10-15 minutes. Serve warm or cold. This makes an excellent luncheon or supper dish.
